title: Dune and Avengers Movies to Open on Same Day
summary: A new movie called Dune: Part Three is coming out in December 2026. It is the last part of a sci-fi series starring Timothée Chalamet and Zendaya. The trailer shows big space battles and follows the story of Paul Atreides. But there is a problem: another big movie, Avengers: Doomsday, is set to open on the same day. Avengers movies usually make a lot of money, so studios try to avoid this. Dune has already booked special Imax screens for its opening weekend, which means it might not change its date. Warner Bros, the studio behind Dune, released the trailer after winning Oscars. They are showing they are strong even though their company might be sold. The first Dune movie made $402 million, and the second made even more. Fans call this battle 'Dundesday,' but the dates could still change.

a new Marvel movie coming out in December 2026
a short video that shows parts of a movie to get people excited
the place where tickets are sold, or how much money a movie makes

When is Dune: Part Three set to open in theaters?
Which other movie might open on the same day as Dune: Part Three?
What special screens has Dune: Part Three booked for its opening weekend?
Which studio made the Dune movies?
What do fans call the box office battle between Dune and Avengers?

background_read: Movies are big stories shown in theaters. Sometimes, two popular movies open on the same day, which can be exciting for fans but tricky for studios because they want to make money. Dune is a sci-fi series about space and battles, while Avengers is a superhero series. Studios like Warner Bros make movies and try to pick good release dates. Trailers help people get excited before a movie comes out.
Article_Structure: WHO: Timothée Chalamet, Zendaya, Warner Bros, Disney
WHAT: Dune: Part Three and Avengers: Doomsday might open on the same day in December 2026
WHERE: In theaters worldwide, especially in the US with Imax screens
WHY: To show a box office battle and how studios compete, with Dune showing strength after Oscar wins

perspectives.description: excited to see two big movies on the same day and call it 'Dundesday'
